name: Berkshire County Regional Housing Authority-H
description_short: UpSide413 (formerly Berkshire County Regional Housing Authority) provides non-profit housing counseling, legal services, and dispute resolution to promote household stability across Berkshire County and Pioneer Valley.
description_long:
UpSide413, based in Pittsfield, Massachusetts, is a non-profit organization formerly known as the Berkshire County Regional Housing Authority. The organization was established to address housing instability and community conflicts through evidence-based support services. Their rebranding to UpSide413 reflects an expansion beyond traditional housing authority functions into comprehensive household and community stability services.

The organization offers a continuum of interrelated services designed to prevent housing loss and strengthen families. Their core offerings include HUD-approved housing counseling, legal services, consumer counseling, intensive case management, tenancy preservation resources, and community-based dispute resolution. They also operate court-connected mediation programs and provide fraud-prevention support. Clients can initiate contact through an online assessment form that connects them with appropriate services based on individual circumstances.

UpSide413 distinguishes itself through its integrated service model that addresses root causes of housing instability rather than symptoms alone. The organization serves not only Berkshire County but extends resources into the Pioneer Valley region, demonstrating a regional commitment. Their inclusion of dispute resolution and mediation services (including a 2026 Basic Mediation Training program) sets them apart from single-function housing authorities. The organization emphasizes community-based solutions alongside court-connected options.

As a non-profit with no commercial lending operations, UpSide413 is genuinely free or low-cost for consumers. However, the organization's capacity and service accessibility depend on funding and geographic location. While they serve multiple counties, availability and wait times may vary. Their effectiveness relies on client follow-through with recommended strategies and active participation in case management.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling services available without commercial lending conflicts
Integrated legal services component addresses both housing and consumer law issues
Covers Berkshire County and extends into Pioneer Valley region for broader accessibility
Court-connected dispute resolution option provides formalized mediation for housing conflicts
Online assessment form allows clients to self-identify needs before contact
Non-profit structure ensures no profit motive driving recommendations
Intensive case management available for clients facing complex housing stability challenges
cons: Service availability and response times not specified on website; may have wait lists due to non-profit capacity constraints
Geographic service area limited to Berkshire County and Pioneer Valley; no services outside this region
No information about whether services are completely free or if sliding-scale fees apply
Website lacks specific details about counselor credentials, certifications, or client outcome data
No online portal or status tracking system mentioned for existing clients
best_for: Renters and homeowners in Berkshire County facing eviction or foreclosure risks
Consumers with housing-related legal disputes or mediation needs
Low-income households seeking HUD-certified housing counseling without commercial pressure
diagnosis: UpSide413 is best for Berkshire County and Pioneer Valley residents facing housing instability, legal housing disputes, or consumer fraud issues who need non-profit, HUD-certified guidance. The main caveat is that service availability depends on non-profit funding and capacity, and the organization cannot help consumers outside its geographic service area.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
